family: CR3BP Earth-Moon DRO/Lyapunov band (C_J ~ 3.00) new-family discovery

method_capability.genome: natural-parameter (Jacobi) continuation of symmetric perpendicular-crossing orbits, single-family from sourced seeds

search_extent.note: points_total ~ n_seeds * 2 directions * 25 steps; the #218/#219 single-family continuation sweep over this Jacobi band

prune_gates: distinct-new-family (not a reproduction or same-family continuation point)
verdict: EMPTY-FOR-NEW-FAMILIES CONDITIONAL ON single-family Jacobi-continuation from sourced symmetric seeds (cr3bp-jacobi-continuation-v1) -- NOT a true void; a METHOD-CAPABILITY limit
interpretation: Our #218/#219 sweep surfaced 0 distinct new families in the Earth-Moon C_J~3.00 DRO/Lyapunov band. This is NOT emptiness of the band: an independent group (arXiv:2509.12671, DA / high-order-transfer-map global multi-revolution fixed-point enumeration over a Poincare section via automatic-domain-splitting + reduced-Poincare-orbit polynomial optimization) DID find a previously-undocumented hybrid DRO/Lyapunov-bridge family (Png'; lowest member P5g' at C_J=3.00022, T=11.175 TU) in exactly this region. Png' is non-symmetric (xdot0 != 0) and a multi-revolution g'-bifurcation -- structurally out of reach for single-family continuation from symmetric perpendicular-crossing seeds, which never enumerates multi-rev fixed points over a domain. The emptiness is therefore a method-capability limit, not a true void. Png' is a CR3BP PERIODIC ORBIT, not a cycler -- it is a cross-check target only, never a catalogue row. RE-OPEN this region when a DA/HOTM-class GLOBAL multi-rev discovery lane ships (capability-subsumption rule: a global-enumeration method strictly subsumes seed-local continuation).
source_anchors: arXiv:2509.12671 (Zhou et al. 2025, HOTM fixed-point search; Table 3 Png' fixed points); cross-check note docs/notes/2026-06-13-high-order-transfer-map-2509.12671-mining.md

family: planet-centric moon system (Uranus + Neptune regular moons)

method_capability.genome: phase-full VILM endgame (leveraging, planet-centric moon tour)

result.reason: all legs unlinkable: contours disjoint at every probed vinf 4-15 km/s

verdict: EMPTY (STRUCTURAL) — no leveraging-VILM moon-tour links; regular-moon Tisserand contours disjoint at all V_inf
interpretation: The Uranian (Miranda/Ariel/Umbriel/Titania/Oberon) and Neptunian (Triton/Proteus) regular moons are too widely-spaced and low-mass for ballistic resonant linking: prune_topology_legs reports every consecutive-moon leg unlinkable (disjoint Tisserand/resonance contours) across vinf seeds 4-15 km/s, even for the tightest adjacent pair (Ariel-Umbriel). The leveraging endgame walks V_inf WITHIN a contour and cannot bridge disjoint contours, so it adds nothing. Verified vs positive controls (Jovian Io-Europa-Ganymede and Saturnian Dione-Rhea-Titan both link at vinf=4). Distinct method capability from the non-leveraging repeated-moon lane that swept the same systems (#254); confirms the same structural emptiness via the leveraging genome.
source_anchors: none (novel registry-clean probe; leveraging-endgame lane never run at Uranus/Neptune before)

family: ballistic precursor-MGA insertion into classic Earth-Mars cyclers

method_capability.genome: epoch-locked Tisserand-Poincare MGA chain, multi-rev Lambert branch selection

verdict: EMPTY (SEARCH-METHOD LIMIT) — the (circular-body Tisserand seed + local TOF optimiser + multi-rev Lambert) method finds no ballistic-gate-passing precursor-MGA into either classic E-M cycler under real DE440 ephemeris; NOT proof of non-existence
interpretation: max_revs=2 IS active (validated median flyby-continuity drops ~2 km/s vs single-rev #302) but the two binding residuals never co-minimise: the best joint candidate (E-V-E) carries closure~3.05 + continuity~4.0 km/s on both targets, both an order of magnitude above the 0.10 km/s ballistic gate. A leg can reach near-ballistic continuity only at the cost of a ~4 km/s terminal V_inf-mismatch. IMPORTANT precision: the closure (close_epoch_locked) already uses real DE440 ephemeris (eccentric bodies); the circular-body assumption is only in the Tisserand SEED enumerator, and the TOF refinement is LOCAL Nelder-Mead. So this is a search-method limit (circular seeds + local optimiser cannot reach a real-eph ballistic basin), NOT proof the cyclers lack a ballistic precursor. Capability-subsumes the #302 single-rev negative (same search box + multi-rev branches). Re-sweep is warranted if a global epoch/geometry search OR an eccentric-body Tisserand seed enumerator is built — those change the method capability. Distinct from ER3BP (#293), which makes the rotating-frame primaries eccentric (different model, different question).
source_anchors: complements #302 Phase-4 precursor-matcher note; consistent with #388/S1L1 family-selection findings (real geometry needs body eccentricity)

family: ballistic/powered precursor-MGA insertion into classic Earth-Mars cyclers

method_capability.genome: unified global MGA-DSM engine: eccentric-aware Tisserand-Poincare sequence enumeration + global differential_evolution over (epoch, per-leg TOFs, per-leg DSM)
method_capability.note: eccentric-radius re-screen (eccentric_tp_linkable_radius_au) is SCAFFOLDED not wired; sequences still enumerated on the circular/mean-a graph. The global search, not the seed eccentricity, was the binding lever.

verdict: CAPABILITY ADVANCE, NOVELTY EMPTY -- the global engine + per-leg DSM collapses the #307 flyby-continuity wall (4 km/s -> ~0) and finds low_maintenance POWERED precursors into both classic E-M cyclers (vinf-match 0.21/0.44 km/s, 75/259 m/s DSM), but 0 literature-fresh and NO clean sub-gate BALLISTIC insertion at the seed V_inf; both rows stay V0
interpretation: Distinct, stronger method than the #307 multi-rev local-optimiser negative (capability-subsumes it). The global differential_evolution over real-DE440 closure with per-leg DSMs reaches geometries the local-from-circular-seed method could not: every survivor has ~0 flyby continuity, and the best Earth-launched chains (Aldrin E-V-V-E, S1L1 E-V-E/E-E) reach within 0.21-0.51 km/s of the cyclers' seed V_inf at low_maintenance DSM cost. BUT: (a) 0 literature-fresh -- every survivor resembles a published anchor (expected: classic E-M precursor space is well-studied); (b) the strictly_ballistic-banded survivors either miss the seed V_inf (E-E-E resonant loops, vinf-match ~6.5) or are non-Earth-launched enumerator artifacts (the engine sequence enumeration does not enforce Earth start). So no NOVEL and no clean BALLISTIC insertion -> no V0-V5 gauntlet candidate (gauntlet is for novel rows). Honest discipline note: the run summary's headline 'closure 0.0022 strictly_ballistic' is the engine's internal closure vs the candidate's OWN seed bins on a non-Earth-launched V-E chain, NOT a match to the cycler -- the insertion metric is vinf_match_residual_kms. Re-sweep warranted if: Earth-launch is enforced in enumeration, the eccentric-radius re-screen is wired, or a wider/global epoch search is run.
source_anchors: supersedes precursor-mga-into-aldrin-s1l1-multirev-2026-06-23 (#307); complements #302 Phase-4 note; spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-23-global-precursor-engine-design.md

family: ER3BP rotating-frame cyclers continued from CR3BP families into e>0

method_capability.genome: #293 ER3BP genome (pulsating-rotating EOM + periodic corrector + eccentricity continuation) used for DISCOVERY (first time)
method_capability.corrector: continue_er3bp_family_in_e (e=0->target) + full-period Floquet monodromy per step; classify survives/dies/bifurcates by elliptic<->hyperbolic stability TRANSITION

search_extent.seed_source: Broucke 1969 7P + Koblick 2023 NRHO (Earth-Moon only; no high-e Sun-planet CR3BP seed IC available)

result.note: all 5 seeds stay hyperbolic across all 61 steps and reach target e (EM 0.0549; EM extended to 0.15) with no stability transition

verdict: NEGATIVE (CONDITIONAL) — the (eccentricity-continuation + Floquet-transition) method finds NO novel e>0-only ER3BP cycler branching off the Broucke/Koblick Earth-Moon families up to e=0.15; the known CR3BP families simply persist into the ER3BP. Capability delivered + golden-validated.
interpretation: First discovery use of the #293 ER3BP genome. 5/5 seeded CR3BP rotating-frame cycler families (Broucke 1969 7P + 2 Koblick 2023 NRHO, all Earth-Moon) continue smoothly into the ER3BP past the real Earth-Moon eccentricity and on to e=0.15, staying in their unstable/hyperbolic stability regime with no bifurcation -> no e>0-only family branches off. All match the published corpus (0 literature-fresh). IMPORTANT scope: NEGATIVE is conditional. (1) Seed-limited to Earth-Moon -- the high-e Sun-planet systems where the ER3BP departs most from the CR3BP (Mercury 0.206, Mars 0.093, Pluto 0.249) were skipped (no CR3BP seed IC at those mu; ICs NOT fabricated), so the most-promising regime was not reached. (2) Small seed set (3 families). (3) Method is bifurcation-along-continuation; direct-e>0 seeding (families with no CR3BP limit) and branch-switching (to trace any flagged bifurcation) were out of scope. Does NOT establish ER3BP-only cyclers don't exist -- only that this method/coverage found none. Re-sweep warranted with: high-e Sun-planet seeds (generate CR3BP Lyapunov/DRO at those mu first), direct-e>0 seeding, or branch-switching. Two correctness traps were caught before the run (period_f half/full-period; bifurcation detector firing on every stable orbit) -- see verdict note.
source_anchors: Broucke 1969 TR 32-1360 (golden); Koblick 2023 AMOSTECH Table 4; ER3BP scoping docs/notes/2026-06-16-frontier-scoping-er3bp-bcr4bp-3d-qp-epoch.md Axis 1; spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-24-er3bp-discovery-campaign-design.md

family: repeated-moon (moontour) quasi-cyclers, length-3 X-Y-X resonant tours

topologies.note: all length-3 repeated-moon cycles among the 5 moons, 20 templates

method_capability.genome: #320/#344 epoch-aware repeated-moon moontour sweep (Lambert relegs + closure residual + dv_band + physical max-bend gate + resonance/irrationality screen)
method_capability.corrector: scan_433_jupiter_galilean.py: phase-grid x n_rev-grid Lambert closure, offline KNOWN_CORPUS literature_check
method_capability.note: Jupiter Galilean system was UNSWEPT by #320 (which covered Saturn/Neptune/Pluto); this fills that gap. Finer 48x48 phase + wider n_rev<=5 vs #320 24x24 n_rev<=3 (the two levers that improved Saturn in #344).

prune_gates: rotation-number irrationality / resonance screen (Galilean Laplace 1:2:4 floods grid)

result.note: strongest closure (Callisto-Ganymede-Callisto 8.3 m/s, physical PASS) is a KNOWN outer-Galilean resonant tour (6 anchors); every literature-fresh closure involves Amalthea and FAILS the physical max-bend gate (Amalthea too small/deep in Jupiter well to bend a leg enough).

verdict: NEGATIVE -- no novel, physically-feasible, sub-0.05 km/s repeated-moon quasi-cycler in the Jupiter Galilean + Amalthea system at 48x48 phase and n_rev<=5. The one feasible strong closure (Callisto-Ganymede-Callisto, 8.3 m/s) is a published resonant-tour structure (not fresh); all literature-fresh closures (Amalthea-involving) are physically infeasible. Mirrors Saturn/Neptune/Uranus moontour outcomes: the method re-derives the literature tours; the unstudied corners are unstudied because infeasible.
interpretation: Registry-grade negative for novelty. The resonance screen removed 558/700 cells (Galilean Laplace resonance as predicted). Re-sweep warranted only if a NEW capability subsumes this: 3D/inclined relegs (Amalthea is inclined), low-thrust relegs (could rescue the fresh-but-infeasible Amalthea cycles), or a finer phase grid if a near-gate fresh cycle is later prioritized. Catalogue note: Callisto-Ganymede-Callisto is a clean KNOWN-attribution V0 admission candidate if Jovian coverage is later desired (separate follow-on, not a novelty claim).
source_anchors: #320 moontour sweep template; #344 finer-grid lever; spec/plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-24-433-jupiter-galilean-quasicycler-plan.md

method_capability.corrector: continue_er3bp_family_in_e (e=0->target) + full-period Floquet; classify survives/dies/bifurcates by elliptic<->hyperbolic stability TRANSITION
method_capability.note: capability-EXTENDS er3bp-discovery-em-broucke-koblick-2026-06-24 (#432): the Sun-Mercury/Sun-Mars systems listed there as seed_limited_skipped are now PROBED at real e (plus Sun-Pluto). Lyapunov amplitude raised to 3e-3 so the near-L1 orbit is well-conditioned for ER3BP continuation at mu~1e-7.

prune_gates: corrector conditioning (Lyapunov amplitude 3e-3 to avoid near-L1 ill-conditioning at mu~1e-7)

result.note: all 6 seeds reach target real e with no stability transition (e_star=None); no e>0-only family branches off any high-e Sun-planet Lyapunov or DRO family. literature_fresh=2 (Mercury Lyapunov+DRO not-found) is necessary-not-sufficient and moot since no bifurcation/novel structure arose.

verdict: NEGATIVE (CONDITIONAL) -- the (eccentricity-continuation + Floquet-transition) method finds NO novel e>0-only ER3BP family branching off the L1-Lyapunov or DRO families of Sun-Mercury (0.206), Sun-Mars (0.093) or Sun-Pluto (0.249) up to their real eccentricities. The high-departure regime #432 flagged as most-promising-but-unreached is now reached and is EMPTY under this method. CR3BP seed generator at arbitrary mu delivered + EM-golden-validated.
interpretation: Closes the #432 seed-limitation: the high-e Sun-planet systems are now probed at real e and yield 6/6 survives, 0 bifurcations. Remaining open conditions: (a) seed families are libration (Lyapunov) + co-orbital (DRO), adjacent-to not strictly cycler-class (this is where the published ER3BP literature lives, so the right frontier, but a high-e cycler-class resonant seed is still untested); (b) method is bifurcation-along-continuation -- a family with NO CR3BP limit needs direct-e>0 seeding (#436), and a flagged bifurcation needs branch-switching (#436, none arose to switch on). Does NOT establish ER3BP-only cyclers do not exist -- only that this method/coverage found none across EM + 3 high-e Sun-planet systems. Harvest correctness note: first run reported Mercury/Mars Lyapunov dies@e=0 -- a corrector ill-conditioning artifact (near-L1 tiny orbit at mu~1e-7, err plateaued ~1e-7 vs 1e-10 tol), NOT physical death; fixed by amplitude 3e-3, both now survive.

verdict: NEGATIVE (CONDITIONAL) -- z0-lift + pseudo-arclength finds NO novel out-of-plane cycler beyond the already-known Braik-Ross (1,1) 3D extension (=#287 spike) and the L1 vertical-Lyapunov family, at the ONE planar root currently machine-loadable (C11a). Capability delivered + spike-validated.
interpretation: Conditional on a narrow seed set: catalogue rows carry heliocentric mission elements not rotating-frame CR3BP ICs, so only the C11a (1,1) planar root was seedable (hardcoded from the #287 on-disk baseline) plus the L1 vertical mode. The campaign maps the 3D extensions of one planar cycler + the vertical-Lyapunov family -- both KNOWN. The realistic novel frontier (OTHER planar Aldrin roots lifted to 3D; other systems) was not reached for lack of machine-extractable seeds, NOT because it is empty. lit-check not-found is necessary-not-sufficient and here a sparse-corpus false-negative. Re-sweep when the full planar-cycler set is machine-loadable (the real unlock) or for Sun-planet/other-moon systems.

method_capability.genome: #293 ER3BP corrector + NEW direct_e_seed_grid (CR3BP-independent) + classify_no_cr3bp_limit (reverse-continue to e=0) + branch_at_saddle_center_er3bp (contingent)
method_capability.corrector: converge_direct_seed (single-shot 2-var symmetric) + continue_er3bp_family_in_e_partial (secant predictor) reverse to e=0
method_capability.note: DISCRIMINATOR UNRELIABLE: classify_no_cr3bp_limit flips cr3bp_continuous<->e_only_candidate on continuation step count (n_steps 30/60/90) and run-to-run (BLAS noise). Needs a fold-aware pseudo-arclength tracker.

result.note: 188 e_only_candidate flags are ARTIFACTS: same orbit re-classifies cr3bp_continuous(n30)->e_only(n60)->cr3bp_continuous(n90), and is run-to-run nondeterministic at fixed n_steps. The reverse-continuation discriminator does not reliably distinguish no-CR3BP-limit from CR3BP-continuous. No reliable discovery.

verdict: NEGATIVE (METHODOLOGICAL) -- the direct-e>0 blind-grid + secant reverse-continuation discriminator is UNRELIABLE; its e_only_candidate flag is dominated by continuation step-size + BLAS artifacts (demonstrated: a single EM orbit flips classification across n_steps 30/60/90 and run-to-run). The 188 raw flags are NOT novel families. Scrutiny prevented a false-positive discovery claim. Capability built; discriminator needs a robust tracker.
interpretation: Closes the last untested ER3BP discovery mechanism. With #432 (continuation-from-CR3BP, 0 bifurcations) and #435 (high-e Sun-planet seeds, 6/6 survive), all three mechanisms now exercised and none yields a reliable novel e>0-only cycler. Red flags forced scrutiny: 94% flag rate + clean mu-ordering (EM 0/69 continuous vs Pluto 10/11). Decisive test: identical orbit re-classified at n_steps 30/60/90 flips cr3bp_continuous<->e_only_candidate, plus run-to-run nondeterminism at fixed n_steps -> the death is secant-corrector fragility tracking arbitrary blind-grid orbits across a wide e-range, not a physical family boundary. FIX (follow-on): fold-aware pseudo-arclength family continuation (as #434 uses) before the no-CR3BP-limit test can be trusted. Seed grid + branch-switcher are ready to feed a robust tracker.

verdict: CANDIDATE-POSITIVE (gated) -- broadening the 3D lift to all 4 Braik-Ross cyclers RETIRES the #434 seed-limited caveat and surfaces the strongest 3D-cycler novelty candidates yet: STABLE out-of-plane families off the differently-wound C21 (2,1) and C32 (3,2) planar cyclers, with new (k1,k2,k_z) topologies. NOT a confirmed discovery: they are 3D extensions of KNOWN planar cyclers, Earth-Moon (not coordinate-matchable to the sparse 3D corpus), and need the 3D V0-V5 gauntlet (#306) + a real Earth-Moon-3D lit-check before any claim.
interpretation: Supersedes 3d-broken-plane-em-aldrin-lift-2026-06-24 (#434, C11a-only). The C11a-only run found only the known #287 spike + vertical-Lyapunov; the 4-cycler broadening reaches the C21/C32 territory and finds STABLE out-of-plane resonant-cycler extensions -- the prize (stable 3D cyclers are rare). All 2260 members are closure-verified genuine POs. Gating next step: 3D V0-V5 gauntlet (#306, pending) on the stable C21/C32 families + acquire an Earth-Moon 3D coordinate catalogue (Breakwell & Brown 1979 / JPL 3-body DB) to turn the lit-check from sparse-false-negative into real adjudication. Scrutiny: confirm the (66,0,136) C32 family is a continuation artifact; independently re-verify a sample of the stable members.

result.jpl_note: JPL EM 2:1 resonant family is PLANAR (z~1e-20), period ~6 TU, stable; candidate is z=-0.21, period 18.17 TU, C=3.026 -> NOT that family (3D + 3x period). Braik-Ross C21 (18 TU) != JPL 2:1 resonant (6 TU) despite shared label.
result.artifact_excluded: C32 z0_0.10 (66,0,136) confirmed artifact: jacobi C=10.58 (non-physical, cyclers at C~3), extreme winding from continuation-wander

verdict: CANDIDATE STRENGTHENED -- the stable C21 (2,0,10) out-of-plane member is INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED stable (all 6 Floquet eigenvalues on the unit circle, closure 3.6e-12) and does NOT coordinate-match the JPL EM 2:1 resonant family (planar, 6 TU; ours is 3D z=-0.21, 18 TU). Preliminary novelty signal. The (66,0,136) suspect is confirmed a continuation artifact (C=10.58) and excluded. Still NOT a confirmed discovery: full novelty needs the 3D V0-V5 gauntlet (#306) + a complete JPL/Breakwell EM-3D family sweep (only the 2:1 resonant branch checked here).
interpretation: Firms the #438 finding (3d-broken-plane-em-4cycler-lift-2026-06-25). The strongest 3D-cycler candidate (stable C21 extension) survives independent stability re-verification AND a first lit-check: it is not the standard JPL planar 2:1 resonant family. Next: (a) run #306 3D V0-V5 gauntlet; (b) complete the JPL EM-family sweep (halo/vertical/axial/other resonant branches near C=3.026, T=18 TU) + Breakwell-Brown 1979 to fully adjudicate; (c) confirm whether the Braik-Ross C21 planar cycler itself is in JPL. If novel + gauntlet-passing, catalogue-admit via the Umbriel template.

method_capability.genome: #444 scrutiny — JPL Three-Body Periodic Orbit DB API full EM-family sweep

search_extent.match_criterion: period within ~1-1.5 TU of 18.167 AND jacobi within 0.06 of 3.0258 AND |z|>0.05

result.closest: resonant 13 & 23 overlap period (16.5-18.9 TU) but jacobi maxes ~2.84-2.97 < 3.026 and are PLANAR; all 3D families (halo/vertical/axial) are short-period (0.7-6 TU)
result.note: candidate matches NO JPL EM family. Necessary-not-sufficient for novelty (JPL not exhaustive of all families; Braik-Ross cyclers may be absent from JPL entirely; this is a 3D extension of a KNOWN planar cycler so novelty = novel OUT-OF-PLANE structure).

verdict: NOVELTY SIGNAL ROBUST (necessary-not-sufficient) -- the independently-verified stable C21 (2,0,10) out-of-plane candidate (C=3.026, T=18.17 TU, z=-0.21) matches NO Earth-Moon family across the full JPL three-body catalogue sweep (halo/vertical/axial/7 resonant branches). Combined with verified Floquet stability + machine closure, this is the strongest novel-3D-cycler-structure candidate the project has produced. REMAINING gates before a discovery claim: (1) 3D V0-V5 gauntlet (#306); (2) publication-novelty via literature_check + Breakwell-Brown 1979; (3) confirm Braik-Ross C21 itself absent from JPL.
interpretation: Completes the JPL lit-check flagged as a gap in the prior entry (which only checked the 2:1 resonant branch). The full EM-family sweep is NEGATIVE -- no catalogued family carries an out-of-plane orbit at the candidate period (18 TU) AND Jacobi (3.026). The candidate is a genuine, well-scrutinized stable 3D cycler extension absent from the standard catalogue. Next: build #306 (3D V0-V5 gauntlet) -- it is now concretely motivated by a verified, lit-fresh candidate, not a hypothetical.
